shared_properties: Project core palette keeps primary #D6D0C4, background #0D0D0F, and accent #E07B39.
Floor art keeps a 16px tile cadence so lane assembly stays deterministic.
Collision top edge remains flat and readable at 1px thickness.
differences: Strip size is 144x16px to match the long low-friction floor spans in the GDD.
Icy highlight streaks bias toward the center to communicate slide momentum rather than grip.
Palette emphasizes #4A90A4 and deep slate contrast so the surface reads colder than the wall strip.

context.usage: Primary low-friction floor used to surge the player through fast descent sections.

shape.silhouette: Long flat floor strip with icy highlight band.
shape.collision_read: Top edge stays flat while interior pixels imply low traction.

acceptance_criteria: The slippery highlight band stays continuous across all 16px tile seams.
The surface reads lower-friction than the mossy floor through cooler hue and fewer dark grip pixels.

reference_images.label: slip_down_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds stone texture breakup, low-key lighting, and vertical shaft composition for the active environment asset.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Tilemap-backed strip or repeated SpriteBundle instances aligned to floor lanes.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load once during the Loading state and reuse it for every slick floor segment in the shaft.
runtime_notes: Use Bevy 0.13 with ImagePlugin::default_nearest() so every asset stays pixel-perfect at 4x upscale.
Map this art to the 0.1 friction gameplay surface rather than baking logic into the sprite.
Keep the collision top edge on whole pixels so the player feet never jitter.
